prose:
Method provenance block
Property: Atom-resolved Fukui functions (f+ electrophilic susceptibility, f- nucleophilic susceptibility) for ferrocene; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f+.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding)
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied geometry (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz); no geometry optimization performed (fukui = GFN2-xTB//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral molecule, charge 0, multiplicity 1; cation state (+1, doublet) and anion state (-1, doublet) generated internally for the finite-difference Fukui analysis.
Software/backend: xtb via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (xtb backend; exact version as bundled with chemkit, not explicitly reported by engine).
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF single-point calculations (N, N+1, N-1) converged; integrity.status = "ok" and integrity.trustworthy = true. Engine issued three warnings (reproduced verbatim below). Density-fitting is intrinsic to GFN2-xTB. Implicit solvation was not used; calculation is strictly gas-phase.

Headline numerical result
Most electrophilic site: atom 11 (Fe), Fukui f₊ = 0.22885456 (dimensionless)
Most nucleophilic site: atom 11 (Fe), Fukui f₋ = 0.25938191 (dimensionless)
